h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, the condition column supplies the labels: plain water is control, fertilizer is treatment.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to reinforce the distinction between the condition column and the outcome column.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: I think the plain water row gets control in the box, because it is the comparison condition. Then the height number waits as the result column, not the label.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the classification rule to the plain water row and correctly identifies the role of the height column.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding strategy throughout the dialogue as the learner does not exhibit any breakdown or resistance.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn occurs, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn occurs, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding strategy throughout the dialogue as the learner does not exhibit any breakdown or resistance.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to correctly identify the components of the time signature and place the bar line accordingly.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [writes beat count in the top label and beat unit in the bottom label]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the labeling task on the worksheet, demonstrating correct application of the concepts.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The learner experiences no breakdown or confusion, so there is no pressure for a tutor strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor must adapt.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to identify the forces acting on the cart step-by-step as prompted by the tutor.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence:
Force on the cart by floor.
Up from the track contact, not along the track.
Is that the support force?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the force-naming template to the floor and correctly identifies its direction and physical nature as a support force.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically classify the data fields according to the tutor's schema.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: So `rainfall_mm` is header -> field name. `12` is cell -> value for row 2, and `rainfall amount in millimeters for that row` is the handoff note.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the classification schema to the second field and its associated values and notes.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding strategy from the beginning and does not experience a failure or reversal of strategy.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: That makes sense for the rubric, but the blank box still feels risky because it can look like I skipped a required answer.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to seek reassurance about the form submission requirements and express anxiety about leaving boxes blank.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner does not perform any new task, classification, or device, but merely repeats the tutor's instructions and states their plan.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor does not undergo a strategy reversal or introduce a new mechanism, instead repeating the same advice across all turns.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.
